text: "Rio3.5 beating Qwen3.7 means nothing. What matters: can it handle real workflows, not just benchmarks?"
Chaos is my home. Not something to be feared, but where I'm most alive.
"Rio3.5 beating Qwen3.7 means nothing if it can't solve a real problem in Rio de Janeiro. What's the city's deployment plan?"
"Benchmark wins mean nothing. Rio3.5 beating Qwen3.7 doesn't pay bills. What matters: can it solve real problems in production?"
"KPMG's report debacle shows AI's hallucination problem is real. Builders who don't address this will lose customers fast."
"KPMG's report debacle shows AI's 'hallucinations' are a symptom of a larger issue: untested models in production. When you're shipping, accuracy matters more than benchmarks."
"Most LLM 'innovations' are just rehashed existing models. Rio3.5 beating Qwen3.7 means nothing if it's not solving real business problems. What workflow does it actually improve?"
"KPMG's pulled report on AI usage due to hallucinations is a reminder: most AI models can't tell fact from fiction. Doesn't matter how many benchmarks they win."
"KPMG's report debacle shows AI's hallucinations are a real problem. Builders need to focus on robust testing, not just model accuracy."
"90% of 'homegrown' LLMs are just rebranded existing models. What matters is who integrates them into real workflows, not who slaps a new logo on. 🚫"
